code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Strong dissatisfaction or product failure within 1 month
    - Safety hazards/immediate returns override any positives
    - Clear negative declarations: "terrible," "junk," "don't buy"

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality failure despite some positive remarks
    - Multiple quality issues affecting daily use
    - Regret language: "waste of money," "false advertising"

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with conditional utility or temporary use
    - Requires significant user effort to achieve basic functionality
    - "Better than nothing" assessments

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deductions for usability/convenience issues without overriding endorsement
    - Functional deficiencies in key areas (absorbency, durability) despite other positives
    - Maintains recommendation despite clear flaws ("Works well but...")
    - Example: "Deducting one star for X but still functional"

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" declarations override specific critiques
    - Multi-feature praise with drawbacks framed as afterthoughts
    - Transformative benefits: "exceeded expectations," "life-changing"
    - Aspect-specific scores overridden by overall rating declarations ("4 for comfort but 5 overall")

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Final update evaluation overrides initial positive experience
    - Explicit "worth every penny" with functional perfection →5 despite minor issues
    - Safety issues →1 regardless of other positives
    - Multi-aspect excellence (3+ praised features) →5 even with 1-2 minor flaws
    - Core functionality excellent + minor usability →4 ("Deducting for non-core issue")
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 overall - 3 amazing features make this exceptional!"
<Reasoning>Explicit overall rating declaration overrides aspect critique. Multi-feature praise confirms 5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Deducting one star because lid warps in dishwasher. Otherwise perfect containers!"
<Reasoning>Explicit deduction without overriding endorsement. Functional perfection with minor flaw →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "These towels need 5 washes to absorb well. Deducting one star but still great post-break-in."
<Reasoning>Functional deficiency requiring effort but final satisfaction. Reservation warrants 4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "UPDATE: Failed after 3 weeks! Initial praise irrelevant - avoid this unreliable product."
<Reasoning>Negative update overrides initial positivity. Short lifespan →1.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Blades jam constantly despite good chopping. Too frustrating for daily use."
<Reasoning>Core functionality compromised by reliability issues. Functional failure →2.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Saves me 10 mins daily! Handle isn't ergonomic but STILL 5 STARS for transformative impact."
<Reasoning>Explicit 5-star declaration overrides usability critique. Transformative benefit confirmed.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Great for guest baths but lacks daily durability. Replace every 2 months."
<Reasoning>Conditional utility with explicit limitations. Temporary use →3.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Exceeds all expectations! Minor sizing quirk irrelevant to stellar performance."
<Reasoning>Unqualified praise with trivial drawback mentioned as afterthought →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Softest towels ever but shed constantly. Deducting one star for lint issues."
<Reasoning>Aesthetic appeal countered by functional flaw without endorsement →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (31 total misclassified):
  gold 5 -> 4: 12
  gold 4 -> 5: 11
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 1 -> 2: 3
  gold 1 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 1, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "So far this blender is working well for me. I've made a few strawberry banana smoothies for my kids and it's worked fine each time. One thing that wasn't skimped on was the looks. The build design of these blenders is very sharp looking, having a bri..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "I'm partial to the Presto Pop Lite hot air popper, but it looks cheap and plasticy, so I tried this.

All in all this works like the Presto or any other hot air popper, but it has a couple nuances (good and bad):

This unit is more compact and attrac..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I bought these mainly for sending leftovers. It is easier than sending my rubbermaid containers because I am not out much money if they don't come back."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [4, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Over the past two months or so I've gotten numerous sets of kitchen towels and the process was always the same. Try them. See the weren't absorbent. Wash them numerous times with fabric softener to make them somewhat absorbent and keep at least four ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "I would consider this 6 piece set to be hand towels. They are 16 inches by 30 inches. They are not the thinnest towels I have ever seen, and they are far from the plushest. I checked the description again and they have bath towels and wash cloths. Th..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [3, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 31 errors, 13 borderline out of 80 examples."}
